title: Fatty Springy Egyptian Hake Tagine
description: A rich and exotic Egyptian-inspired dish featuring hake, kombu, and dates. Perfect for a low-carb date-night dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped garlic and grated ginger. Cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped green chili peppers and cook for 2 minutes, until tender.
Add the cumin, coriander, and hake spice blend. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the date syrup, water, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine, then add the hake fillets.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Bake for 12 minutes, or until the hake is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
While the hake cooks, toast the pistachios in a dry skillet over medium heat, until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and cook for 1 minute, until wilted.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the chopped parsley, breadcrumbs, and butter.
Serve the hake hot, garnished with the toasted pistachios and a drizzle of tahini sauce.
To make the tahini sauce, combine the tahini, lemon juice, and water in a blender or food processor.
Blend until smooth, then season with salt to taste.
Serve the sauce on the side or spoon it over the hake.
Garnish with chopped parsley, pistachios, and cilantro, if desired.
